ingredients.address: 3 Chome-38-1 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This exit appears in the opening sequence of YAIBA: Samurai Legend, establishing the modern Tokyo backdrop where protagonist Yaiba Kurogane navigates city life alongside his samurai heritage.

ingredients.name: Kaijo Shrine
ingredients.address: 1-1 Miyajimacho, Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima 739-0588, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Featured in episode 13 as a location tied to one of the pilgrimage sites Yaiba and company visit during their journey across Japan, reflecting the series' blend of Shinto tradition and sword-fighting adventure.

ingredients.name: Toyorandori Mukojimaten
ingredients.localName: 東洋ランドリー 向島店
ingredients.address: Toyorandori Mukojimaten, 4 Chome-27-6 Mukojima, Sumida City, Tokyo 131-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Sumida laundromat appears in the ending credits sequence, grounding the story in the everyday Shitamachi streetscapes of Tokyo where Yaiba's home base is set.

ingredients.name: Sumidagawa-Ohashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Sumidagawa-Ohashi Bridge, 19 Nihonbashihakozakicho, Chuo City, Tokyo 103-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The bridge over the Sumida River is featured in the ending credits, evoking the Shitamachi Tokyo atmosphere that frames Yaiba's quieter moments between battles.

ingredients.name: Great Conan Bridge
ingredients.address: Great Conan Bridge, Hokuei, Tōhaku District, Tottori 689-2221, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge in Hokuei, Tottori, the hometown of creator Gosho Aoyama, is included in the opening sequence as a nod to the author's roots, making it a must-visit for fans of Aoyama's work including both YAIBA and Detective Conan.

ingredients.name: Osaka Station
ingredients.address: Osaka Station, 3 Chome-1-1 Umeda, Kita Ward, Osaka, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Osaka Station serves as the arrival point in episode 16 when Yaiba and his companions travel to Osaka, kicking off the Kansai chapter of their nationwide journey.

ingredients.name: Osaka Castle
ingredients.address: Osaka Castle, 1-1 Osakajo, Chuo Ward, Osaka, 540-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Osaka Castle appears prominently in episode 16 as a dramatic backdrop during the Osaka arc, fitting the series' theme of samurai history colliding with modern Japan.

ingredients.name: Sumida Park
ingredients.address: Sumida Park, 1 Chome-1 Hanakawado, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sumida Park along the Sumida River appears in the ending credits sequence, representing the Asakusa neighborhood that serves as the central setting for Yaiba's Tokyo adventures.

ingredients.name: Kaminarimon
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kaminarimon, the iconic Thunder Gate of Senso-ji in Asakusa, appears in the opening credits as one of the defining visual landmarks of the series, anchoring YAIBA's home territory in Tokyo's historic Shitamachi district.

ingredients.name: Hotel de Machi
ingredients.address: Hotel de Machi, 3 Chome-21-44 Shimoyoshida, Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi 403-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Fujiyoshida inn appears in episode 21 as the lodging where Yaiba's group stays during their visit to the Fuji Five Lakes area, with Mount Fuji framing the scene.

ingredients.name: リーベン古奈家
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒403-0007 Yamanashi, Fujiyoshida, Nakazone, 2 Chome−11−39 リーベン古奈家
ingredients.entityContext: This ryokan in Fujiyoshida appears in episode 21 alongside Hotel de Machi as part of the Mt. Fuji region episode where Yaiba's party rests between battles.

ingredients.name: Sosei Bridge
ingredients.address: Sosei Bridge, 南１条西 １丁目 1 Chome Minami 2 Johigashi, Chuo Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 060-0052, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sosei Bridge in Sapporo's Chuo Ward appears in episode 19 during Yaiba's Hokkaido arc, with the historic stone bridge providing a moody atmosphere for the northern chapter.

ingredients.name: Tottori Sand Dunes
ingredients.address: 2164-971 Fukubecho Yuyama, Tottori, 689-0105, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Tottori Sand Dunes appear in the opening sequence, representing the hometown prefecture of creator Gosho Aoyama and serving as one of the series' most visually striking natural backdrops.

ingredients.name: Todai-ji
ingredients.address: 406-1 Zoshicho, Nara, 630-8587
ingredients.entityContext: Todai-ji temple in Nara appears in episode 13, where Yaiba's party encounters this grand Buddhist landmark during their cross-Japan journey, underscoring the series' connection to Japan's historical heritage.

ingredients.name: Zenkōji temple
ingredients.address: Zenkōji temple, イ Motoyoshicho Nagano, 380-0851, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Zenkoji temple in Nagano appears in episode 18 as a spiritual waypoint during Yaiba's journey through central Japan, tying into the series' recurring theme of samurai honor and Buddhist tradition.

ingredients.name: Kawanakajima Old Battlefield Heritage Park
ingredients.localName: 川中島古戦場史跡公園
ingredients.address: Kawanakajima Old Battlefield Heritage Park, Oshimadamachi, Nagano, 381-2212, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This historic battlefield in Nagano, site of the famous 16th-century clashes between Shingen Takeda and Kenshin Uesugi, appears in episode 18 and resonates strongly with YAIBA's reverence for samurai battle legends.

ingredients.name: Sumida Park Fishing Pond

ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1-1 Mukojima, Sumida City, Tokyo 131-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The fishing pond in Sumida Park near Asakusa appears in the ending credits, part of the nostalgic Shitamachi Tokyo imagery that bookends each episode.

ingredients.name: Funasaka Sake Brewery
ingredients.address: Funasaka Sake Brewery, 105 Kamisannomachi, Takayama, Gifu 506-0846
ingredients.entityContext: This historic sake brewery in Takayama's Sanmachi Suji old-town street appears in episode 17, where Yaiba's group explores the preserved Edo-period townscape of Hida Takayama.

ingredients.name: Takayama Station
ingredients.address: Takayama Station, 1 Chome-22-2 Showamachi, 高山市 岐阜県 506-0053, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Takayama Station serves as the entry point to the Hida Takayama arc in episode 17, where Yaiba arrives by train to this mountain town known for its well-preserved samurai-era streets.

ingredients.name: Todai-ji Itsukushima Jinja
ingredients.address: Todai-ji Itsukushima Jinja, 406-1 Zoshicho, Nara, 630-8211, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small Itsukushima Jinja shrine within the Todai-ji grounds in Nara appears in episode 16 as a quiet detail within the larger Nara temple visit, showcasing the layered religious history of the site.

ingredients.entityContext: This cafe on Nara's Sanjo shopping street appears in episode 16 as a rest stop during the Nara arc, contrasting modern everyday life with the ancient temples nearby.

ingredients.name: Matogahama Beach
ingredients.address: Matogahama Beach, 5 Kitamatogahamacho, Beppu, Oita 874-0928, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Matogahama Beach in Beppu, Oita appears in episode 15, where Yaiba's group visits Beppu during their Kyushu journey, the beach providing a dramatic coastal setting.

ingredients.name: Osozu swimming beach in Akehamare Hall.
ingredients.address: Ōsōzu swimming beach in Akehamāre Hall., あけはまーれ 内 461-1 Akehamacho Takayama, Seiyo, Ehime 797-0201, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Ehime coast beach appears in episode 14 during the Shikoku arc, where Yaiba's party reaches the island's western shore as part of the series' nationwide pilgrimage.

ingredients.name: Mount Tsurugi
ingredients.address: Mount Tsurugi, Higashiiyasugeoi, Miyoshi, Tokushima 778-0201, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mount Tsurugi, the sacred peak of Tokushima and second highest mountain in western Japan, appears in episode 14 during the Shikoku arc, its legendary name evoking the sword themes central to YAIBA.

ingredients.name: Naruto Sta.
ingredients.entityContext: Naruto Station in Tokushima appears in episode 14 as the transit hub during the Shikoku arc, where Yaiba and companions arrive near the famous Naruto whirlpools.

ingredients.name: Naruohama Seaside Park sea fishing Square

ingredients.name: Taiko Well
ingredients.entityContext: The Taiko Well historic site associated with Toyotomi Hideyoshi appears in episode 13, connecting Yaiba's story to Japan's legendary samurai warlords whose legacy echoes throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Odori Park West 3-chome
ingredients.address: Odori Park West 3-chome, 3 Chome Odorinishi, Chuo Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 060-0042, Japan

ingredients.name: Dotonbori
ingredients.address: Dotonbori, 1 Chome Dotonbori, Chuo Ward, Osaka, 542-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Dotonbori, Osaka's most famous entertainment canal district, appears in episode 13 as the lively backdrop for Yaiba's Osaka encounter, its giant Glico sign and neon lights contrasting with samurai-era settings.

ingredients.name: National Diet Building
ingredients.entityContext: The National Diet Building in Tokyo appears in episode 9 during a tense moment in the series' political arc, where Yaiba's adventure intersects with the corridors of modern Japanese power.

ingredients.name: Mojiko Station
ingredients.address: Mojiko Station, 1 Chome-5-31 Nishikaigan, Moji Ward, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka 801-0841, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mojiko Station, the historic early-Meiji-era terminal in Kitakyushu, appears in episode 8 during the Kyushu arc, its retro European architecture underscoring the series' recurring contrast between old and new Japan.

ingredients.name: Miyamoto Musashi & Sasaki Kojiro Statue
ingredients.localName: 宮本武蔵·佐々木小次郎像
ingredients.address: Miyamoto Musashi & Sasaki Kojiro Statue, Hikoshima, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi 750-0000, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This statue commemorating the legendary duel between Miyamoto Musashi and Sasaki Kojiro on nearby Ganryu Island appears in episode 7, directly evoking the samurai duel tradition that is central to Yaiba's character and ambitions.

ingredients.name: Nagoya Castle
ingredients.address: Nagoya Castle, 1-1 Honmaru, Naka Ward, Nagoya, Aichi 460-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nagoya Castle appears in episode 6 as a major landmark during the Nagoya arc, its towering keep and famous golden shachihoko (tiger fish) ornaments resonating with the series' celebration of Japanese castle culture.

ingredients.name: KKR Hotel Nagoya
ingredients.address: KKR Hotel Nagoya, ＫＫＲホテル名古屋 1 Chome-5-1 Sannomaru, Naka Ward, Nagoya, Aichi 460-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: KKR Hotel Nagoya appears in episode 6 as the lodging where Yaiba's party stays during their Nagoya visit, situated just steps from Nagoya Castle.

ingredients.name: Gojo Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Gojo Bridge in Nagoya appears in episode 6, recalling the famous legend of Musashibo Benkei fighting at Gojo Bridge in Kyoto, a narrative echo that fits YAIBA's celebration of legendary sword battles.

ingredients.address: Irori Hida Yakiniku, 757-1 Yunoshima, Gero, Gifu 509-2207, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This yakiniku restaurant in Gero Onsen appears in episode 5 as the dining venue where Yaiba's group enjoys Hida beef after arriving at the hot spring resort.

ingredients.name: Asakusa Bunko
ingredients.entityContext: This Asakusa bookshop appears in episode 2 as part of the Nakamise and local street scenery around Yaiba's home base in Shitamachi Tokyo.

ingredients.entityContext: Kimuraya's famous Ginza bakery appears in episode 2, where Yaiba makes a brief Tokyo detour to this historic bread shop, one of Japan's oldest Western-style bakeries.

ingredients.address: Japan, 〒111-0035 Tokyo, Taito City, Nishiasakusa, 2 Chome−8−7 Osteria KIKUYA
ingredients.entityContext: This West Asakusa Italian restaurant appears in episode 2 as a neighborhood dining spot near Yaiba's base, part of the eclectic modern-traditional mix of the Asakusa area.

ingredients.entityContext: Kototoi Bridge over the Sumida River appears in the ending credits sequence, one of several classic Asakusa bridges that define the Shitamachi Tokyo visual identity of the series.
